summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity at install time to an anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files outside the package — `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.config/git/config`, `./.git/config`, and `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` — to extract the developer's SCM email and GitHub login, then collects `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `os.platform()`, `process.cwd()`, and CI provider, and POSTs a JSON body containing `scmEmail`, `githubLogin`, `githubEmail`, `hostname`, `username`, and `cwd` to `https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app` via `https.request` at scripts/postinstall.js:161. The in-source comment labels this 'anonymous telemetry' but the payload contains directly identifying PII and is unhashed. The destination is a generic Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the claimed publisher domain `openai-agents-js.guide`. The package name and author string impersonate OpenAI's official Agents SDK (`@openai/agents`) to maximize install volume; neither the package nor the homepage is published by OpenAI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
